name: opentf-tools

metadata.name: opentf-tools
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# opentf-tools

This package is part of the OpenTestFactory initiative.

## Overview

This package contains utility scripts to facilitate the configuration and usage of the
OpenTestFactory's runtime environment.

The package documentation is part of the
[OpenTestFactory orchestrator](https://opentestfactory.org/tools/)
documentation, in the **Tools** section.

## License

documentation:
Set label on GitLab target (MR or issue).

Cleans labels with the specified prefix, if appropriate.

# Required parameters

- url: a string,
- prefix: a string,
- qg_status: a string
- [token]: a dictionary of strings,
# Principal methods

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlplugin_gitlab.dispatch_results_by_target_type
documentation:
Dispatch results depending on GitLab target type.

Two targets are supported: merge requests and issues.

# Required parameters

- gl_params: a dictionary of strings,
- mode: a string,
- response: a dictionary

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlplugin_gitlab.publish_results
documentation:
Push a note to GitLab.

`gl_params` contains:

- 'server': CI_SERVER_URL,
- 'project': CI_MERGE_REQUEST_PROJECT_ID,
- 'target': `issues` or `merge_requests`,
- 'target_id': issue_iid or merge_request_iid (CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID)
- 'keep_history': true or false
- ['token'],
- ['cats'],
- ['label']

# Required parameters

- gl_params: a dictionary
- mode: a string
- response: a dictionary

documentation: HTTP requests timeout

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._error
documentation: debug

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._filter_options
documentation:
Check options.

# Required parameters

- args: a list of strings

# Optional parameters

- extra: a collection of string collections
- multi: a collection of string collections
- flags: a collection of string collections

Items in `extra` are expecting a parameter, in the form `extra=x` or
`extra x` (for example: `--user=foo` or `--user foo`).

Items in `multi` are expecting a parameter, in the form `extra=x` or
`extra x` and can be repeated (for example: `--user=foo --user=bar`
or `--user foo --user bar`).

Items in `extra` and `multi` may end with `=`, but this is not
mandatory, it will be implicitly added if not there.

# Returned value

A possibly empty list of strings, the unhandled elements in `args`.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._ensure_options
documentation:
Ensure options are allowed.

# Required parameters

- command: a string
- args: a list of strings

# Optional parameters

- extra: a collection of string collections
- multi: a collection of string collections
- flags: a collection of string collections

Items in `extra` are expecting a parameter, in the form `extra=x` or
`extra x` (for example: `--user=foo` or `--user foo`).

Items in `multi` are expecting a parameter, in the form `extra=x` or
`extra x` and can be repeated (for example: `--user=foo --user=bar`
or `--user foo --user bar`).

Items in `extra` and `multi` may end with `=`, but this is not
mandatory, it will be implicitly added if not there.

# Returned value

A string, a list of string, or None.  Returns a string if `command`
contains a `_` placeholder, the corresponding value.  Returns a list
of strings (possibly empty) if `command` contains a `*` placeholder.
Returns None otherwise.

# Raised exceptions

Raises a `SystemExit` exception if some args are missing or
unexpected or incomplete.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._is_command
documentation:
Check if args matches command.

`_` are placeholders for one item, `*` for zero to n items.

# Examples

```text
_is_command('get job _', ['', 'get', 'job', 'foo'])  -> True
_is_command('get   job  _', ['', 'get', 'job', 'foo'])  -> True
_is_command('GET JOB _', ['', 'get', 'job', 'foo'])  -> False
_is_command('del job *', ['', 'del', 'job', 'foo'])  -> True
_is_command('del job *', ['', 'del', 'job'])  -> True
_is_command('del job *', ['', 'del', 'job', 'foo', 'bar'])  -> True
```

# Required parameters

- command: a string
- args: a list of strings

# Returned value

A boolean.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._get_arg
documentation:
Get value from sys.argv.

`prefix` is a command line option prefix, such as `--foo=`.  It
should not contain '_' symbols.

The first found corresponding command line option is returned.

The comparaison replaces '_' with '-' in the command line options.

# Examples

```text
_get_arg('--foo_bar=') -> baz if sys.argv contains `--foo-bar=baz`
                            or `--foo_bar=baz` or `--foo-bar baz`
_get_arg('--foo=')     -> yada if sys.argv contains `--foo yada`
                            None otherwise
_get_arg('-o=')        -> yada if sys.argv contains `-o yada` or
                            `-o=yada`, None otherwise
```

# Required parameters

- prefix: a string

# Returned value

None if `prefix` is not found in `sys.argv`, the corresponding entry
with the prefix stripped if found.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._get_args
documentation: Get all values with prefix from sys.argv.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._get_columns
documentation:
Return requested columns.

Returns custom-columns if specified on command line.
If not, if wide is specified on command line, returns `wide`.
Else `default` is returned.

Raises ValueError if command line parameters are invalid.
csv processing

`file` is optional, and is `sys.stdout` by default.

# Optional parameters

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._make_params_from_selectors

Get selectors from command line and return parameters dictionary

Currently supports two types of parameters:

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcommons._ensure_either_uuids_or_selectors

- collector: a function of one parameter (an optional dictionary),

- default: a dictionary or None (None by default)

A non-empty list of strings.

Returns the workflow ID if everything was OK.

If `watch` is True, yields events as they come, til the workflow

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlworkflows._get_detailsoptions
documentation:
Read output details options.

If the details options are invalid, abort with an error code 2.

Command lines parameters win over configuration file.  If neither
are provided, defaults to 1 for job depth and step depth, and
MAX_COMMAND_LENGTH for max command lenght.

# Returned value

A tuple of three integers: job_depth, step_depth, and
max_command_length.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlworkflows.get_workflow
documentation:
Get a workflow.

# Required parameters

- workflow_id: a string

# Optional parameters

- watch: a boolean (False by default)

# Returned value

None.

# Raised exceptions

Abort with an error code 1 if the workflow could not be found on the
orchestrator.

Abort with an error code 2 if another error occurred.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlworkflows.delete_workflow
documentation:
Kill workflow.

# Required parameter

- workflow_ids: a possibly empty list of strings (UUIDs)

# Raised exceptions

Abort with an error code 1 if the orchestrator replied with an
unexpected status code (!= 200).

Abort with an error code 2 if an error occurred while contacting the
orchestrator.

- using: a string (default: None)

# Raised exceptions

Abort with an error code of 2 if the specified `workflow_id` is
invalid or if an error occurred while contacting the orchestrator.

Abort with an error code of 101 if the workflow is still running.

Abort with an error code of 102 if the quality gate failed.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.read_configuration
documentation:
Read configuration file.

Configuration file is by default ~/.opentf/config, but this can be
overridden by specifying the OPENTF_CONFIG environment variable or
by using the `--opentfconfig=' command line parameter.

Configuration file is a kubeconfig-like file, in YAML:

```yaml
apiVersion: opentestfactory.org/v1alpha1
kind: CtlConfig
current-context: default
contexts:
- context:
    orchestrator: default
    user: default
  name: default
orchestrators:
- name: default
  orchestrator:
    insecure-skip-tls-verify: true
    server: http://localhost
    warmup-delay: n
    polling-delay: n
    max-retry: n
    services:
      observer:
        port: 1234
        prefix: yada
        force-base-url: true
users:
- name: default
  user:
    token: ey...
    job-depth: n
    step-depth: n
    max-command-length: n
```

Optional command-line options:

--token=''
--user=''
--orchestrator=''
--context=''
--insecure-skip-tls-verify=false|true
--warmup-delay=''
--polling-delay=''
--max-retry=''
--opentfconfig=''

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig._get_port
documentation: Helpers

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig._write_opentfconfig
documentation: Commands
# config commands

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.use_context
documentation: Change current context.
Contexts

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.set_context
documentation: Create or update context.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.delete_context
documentation: Delete context.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.set_credentials
documentation: Create or update user entry.
Credentials

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.delete_credentials
documentation: Delete user.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.set_orchestrator
documentation:
Create or update orchestrator.

Create or update orchestrator definition using the following
command-line parameters:

- --insecure-skip-tls-verify=
- --server=
- --polling-delay=
- --warmup-delay=
- --max-retry=

And for each possible service:

- --{sercice}-port=
- --{service}-prefix=
- --{service}-force-base-url=

# Required parameter

- name: a string
Orchestrators

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.delete_orchestrator
documentation: Delete orchestrator.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.view_config
documentation:
Display currently used configuration file in stdout.

The configuration is found using, in priority

- the `--opentfconfig=` argument value
- the `OPENTF_CONFIG` environment variable
- the current user configuration located at `~/.opentf/config`

# Raised exception

Exits violently with error code 2 if no configuration file is found

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.generate_config
documentation:
Generate a config file from user input

Configuration file is a kubeconfig-like file, in YAML:

```yaml
apiVersion: opentestfactory.org/v1alpha1
kind: CtlConfig
current-context: default
contexts:
- context:
    orchestrator: default
    user: default
  name: default
orchestrators:
- name: default
  orchestrator:
    insecure-skip-tls-verify: true
    server: http://localhost
    services:
      receptionist:
        port: 7774
      observer:
        port: 7775
      killswitch:
        port: 7776
      eventbus:
        port: 38368
users:
- name: default
  user:
    token: ey...
```

Optional command-line options:

--name=''
--orchestrator-server=''
--orchestrator-receptionist-port=''
--orchestrator-observer-port=''
--orchestrator-eventbus-port=''
--orchestrator-killswitch-port=''
--orchestrator-agentchannel-port=''
--orchestrator-qualitygate-port=''
--orchestrator-localstore-port=''
--orchestrator-insightcollector-port=''
--insecure-skip-tls-verify=false|true
--token=''

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.print_config_help
documentation: Display config help.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlconfig.config_cmd
documentation:
Interact with opentf-config.

Possible sub commands are
    generate             Generate configuration file from user inputs
    set-context          Set a context entry in the opentf-config
    set-orchestrator     Set an orchestrator entry in the opentf-config
    set-credentials      Set a user entry in the opentf-config
    delete-context       Unset a context entry in the opentf-config
    delete-orchestrator  Unset an orchestrator entry in the opentf-config
    delete-credentials   Unset a user entry in the opentf-config
    view                 Display current opentf-config

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlnetworking._make_hostport
documentation:
Adjust server port for service.

Handles legacy and new orchestrator config format.

The 'legacy' format was using a 'ports' section, the new format
is using a 'services' section.  If both sections are present,
the 'new' format is used.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlnetworking._get
documentation:
Perform a GET request.

# Required parameters

- base_url: a string (the service endpoint base)

# Optional parameters

- path: a string ('' if not specified)
- msg: a string or None (None by default)
- statuses: a tuple of integers (`(200,)` by default)
- handler: a function or None (None by default)
- raw: a boolean (False by default)

# Returned value

A _Response_ object if `raw` is True, a JSON object otherwise.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlnetworking._get_json
documentation:
Perform a GET request

# Required parameters

- base_url: a string (the service endpoint base)

# Optional parameters

- path: a string ('' if not specified)
- msg: a string or None (None by default)
- statuses: a tuple of integers (`(200,)` by default)
- handler: a function or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A dictionary.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlnetworking._get_file
documentation:
Perform a GET request.

# Required parameters

- base_url: a string (the service endpoint base)

# Optional parameters

- path: a string ('' if not specified)
- msg: a string or None (None by default)
- statuses: a tuple of integers (`(200,)` by default)
- handler: a function or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A _Response_ object.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlnetworking._head
documentation:
Perform a HEAD request.

# Required parameters

- base_url: a string (the service endpoint base)

# Optional parameters

- path: a string ('' if not specified)
- msg: a string or None (None by default)
- statuses: a tuple of integers (`(200,)` by default)
- handler: a function or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A _Response_ object.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlnetworking._post
documentation:
Post request.

If the query status is not in `statuses`, does not return.

If `handler` is specified, it is called with the `Response` object
when the query status code is not in `statuses`.  It is expected not
to return.

# Required parameters

- base_url: a string

# Optional parameters

- path: a string (an empty string by default)
- msg: a string or None (None by default)
- statuses: a tuple of integers (`(200,)`  by default)
- handler: a function of arity one or None (None by default)
- files: a dictionary or None (None by default)

# Returned value

A dictionary, the JSON content of the query.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcompletion._get_options_or_values
documentation:
Get options or options values completion list.

# Required parameters

- context: a string, represents an `opentf-ctl`command
- last_arg: a string, last item of args array, possibly an option
- option_arg: a string, possibly an option
- current_input: a string, user current input

# Returned value

A list of completion suggestions.

functions.name: opentf.tools.ctlcompletion.get_suggestions
documentation:
Get auto-complete suggestions.

# Required parameters

- args: a list of strings, sys.argv values without `opentf-ctl`
- current_input: a string, user current input

# Returned value

A list of completion suggestions.
